channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Judge delays order in antitrust case requiring Google to open up its app store
item.description: A federal judge has delayed an order requiring Google to open up its Android app store to more competition.
item.dc:creator: Michael Liedtke, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: States grasping the benefits of, need for cyber harmonization
item.description: Public and private sector experts say programs like StateRAMP make their data and networks safer as cyber threats become more serious and resources sparse. The post States grasping the benefits of, need for cyber harmonization first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: Tesla software under investigation after collisions
item.description: The National Highway Traffic Safety Administration (NHTSA) opened an investigation into Tesla’s Full Self-Driving (FSD) software on Thursday after the system was reportedly involved in four crashes, including one that killed a pedestrian.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: US to probe Tesla's 'Full Self-Driving' system after pedestrian killed in low visibility conditions
item.description: The U.S. government’s road safety agency is investigating Tesla’s “Full Self-Driving” system after getting reports of crashes in low-visibility conditions, including one that killed a pedestrian.
item.dc:creator: Tom Krisher, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Augmented reality screens are a feature, not a product
item.description: Apple released a claustrophobic submarine movie for Apple Vision Pro earlier this month, and it gave me a sinking feeling. The movie Submerged was neither augmented reality (AR) nor virtual reality (VR) nor 360-degree video. It was more like 180-degree video.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: 7 Android 15 features you can bring to any phone today
item.description: No Android 15? No problem. You can still enjoy some of the fancy new features from Google’s latest Android version — no matter who made your current device or how old it might be.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Computación cuántica para optimizar la red de distribución eléctrica de i-DE en España
item.description: La distribuidora eléctrica del grupo Iberdrola, i-DE, colabora con Ayesa en el desarrollo de una solución basada en tecnología cuántica de cara a optimizar la red eléctrica de España.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: New Chrome extension aims to detect deepfake AI-generated voices—but it’s far from perfect
item.description: Deepfakes have already been a problem in the 2024 presidential election—and could potentially become a bigger one as November 5 draws near.
item.dc:creator: Chris Morris, Fast Company

item.title: Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei pens a smart look at our AI future
item.description: Welcome to AI Decoded, Fast Company’s weekly newsletter that breaks down the most important news in the world of AI. You can sign up to receive this newsletter every week here.
item.dc:creator: Mark Sullivan, Fast Company

item.title: Alabama man arrested in SEC social media account hack that led the price of bitcoin to spike
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — An Alabama man was arrested Thursday for his alleged role in the January hack of a U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission social media account that led the price of bitcoin to spike, the Justice Department said. Eric Council Jr.
item.dc:creator: ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: One Direction was the internet's first boy band, and Liam Payne its grounding force
item.description: One Direction member Liam Payne was found dead this week after falling from a hotel balcony in Buenos Aires, Argentina, at just 31.
item.dc:creator: Maria Sherman, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Netflix's subscriber growth is slowing, but its profit and stock price are still surging
item.description: Netflix says its subscriber growth slowed dramatically during the summer, a sign that the huge gains from the video-streaming service’s crackdown on freeloading viewers is tapering off.
item.dc:creator: Michael Liedtke, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Rep. Tlaib calls out Kroger for facial recognition practices
item.description: Kroger said it uses facial recognition to present targeted ads on its digital price tags that it rolled out five years ago.
item.dc:creator: Kelsey Kernstine, NewsNation

item.title: Breaking down government hacks: The rise of the modern kill chain
item.description: This year, expect to see a record number of cyberattacks on government agencies as hackers ramp up their efforts ahead of the 2024 U.S. presidential election. The post Breaking down government hacks: The rise of the modern kill chain first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: Run a business? Then you need to join this Apple service
item.description: Apple just gave smaller enterprises a chance to compete on a more equal basis with larger concerns with three nice improvements to Apple Business Connect: Business Caller ID, brand identity in Mail, and custom business logos for use with Tap to Pay.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: US agency adopts rule to make it easier for consumers to cancel unwanted subscriptions
item.description: The Federal Trade Commission adopted a final rule Wednesday that will require businesses to make it easy for consumers to cancel unwanted subscriptions and memberships.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Endpoint proliferation exacerbates old cybersecurity problem: data integration
item.description: Newer technologies like automation and AI may offer new solutions to this age-old cybersecurity problem, but they can also be double-edged swords. The post Endpoint proliferation exacerbates old cybersecurity problem: data integration first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Daisy Thornton, Federal News Network

item.title: Cisco pumps up data center networking with AI, large workloads in mind
item.description: Cisco is boosting network density support for its data center switch and router portfolio as it works to deliver the network infrastructure its customers need for cloud architecture, AI workloads and high-performance computing.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: AT&T and Broadcom near settlement in VMware support dispute
item.description: AT&T and Broadcom are close to resolving their legal dispute over VMware support services, according to a joint letter filed in the New York Supreme Court on October 11. The letter requests a postponement of the upcoming hearing, as both sides make progress toward a settlement.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Microsoft settles video gamers' lawsuit over Activision takeover
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Microsoft Corp. has settled a lawsuit from a group of gamers who sued to try to stop the company from buying video game publisher Activision Blizzard for $69 billion last year. Terms of the settlement were not disclosed.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Mexico says foreign firms have pledged $20 billion in investments, but many are old or uncertain
item.description: MEXICO CITY (AP) — Mexican officials announced Tuesday what they claimed was $20 billion in new foreign direct investment in Mexico, but much of that was neither new, nor completely certain.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Apple powers up the iPad mini for Apple Intelligence
item.description: As expected, Apple has introduced a much faster Apple Intelligence-capable iPad mini equipped with the same A17 Pro chip used in the iPhone 15 Pro series. That’s a good improvement from the A15 Bionic in the previous model, and makes for faster graphics, computation, and AI calculation.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: How to manage generative AI programs – governance, education, regulation
item.description: Generative artificial intelligence has grown extremely rapidly, attracting investment and sparking developer interest and creativity. Companies see generative AI as a route to building innovative services and revolutionizing their industries.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Tech consultant goes on trial in death of Cash App founder Bob Lee
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — The tech consultant charged in Cash App founder Bob Lee's stabbing death had no motive to kill him and in fact was forced to defend himself against Lee, who had become aggressive while on a multiday drug bender, lawyers for Nima Momeni said in opening statements Monday.
item.dc:creator: JANIE HAR, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Volunteers bring solar power to Hurricane Helene's disaster zone
item.description: BAKERSVILLE, N.C. (AP) — Nearly two weeks after Hurricane Helene downed power lines and washed out roads all over North Carolina's mountains, the constant din of a gas-powered generator is getting to be too much for Bobby Renfro.
item.dc:creator: GABRIELA AOUN ANGUEIRA, Associated Press, NewsNation
